copy.caption: The biggest animal on land has the world's most remarkable nose.
copy.shortDescription: An elephant's trunk is forty thousand muscles with no bones — soft enough to pick up a single grape, strong enough to uproot a tree. Elephants remember every watering hole, every friend, every loss — a grandmother elephant might carry the map of a landscape she first walked fifty years ago. Fun fact: Elephants make rumbles too low for human ears to hear, sending messages underground through their feet to elephants kilometers away.
